title: Tuareg FLA Launches Drone and Missile Strike on FAMa-Africa Corps Base at Anéfis
detail: The Front de Libération de l'Azawad (FLA), a Tuareg separatist coalition that emerged as a successor to the CSP-PSD (itself a successor to the CMA Algiers Accord signatories), conducted a combined FPV drone and missile strike on a joint FAMa and Africa Corps military compound at Anéfis in Mali's Kidal region. The FLA characterized the strike as its seventh announced operation in March 2026, indicating a sustained asymmetric campaign in the Kidal theater. Both sides disputed damage and casualty claims; no independently verified figures are available. The FLA's escalating drone campaign reflects how former Algiers Accord signatories — displaced from Kidal after the November 2023 FAMa/Africa Corps offensive — are adapting to asymmetric resistance using commercially available drone platforms. The campaign poses a growing challenge to FAMa/Africa Corps control of Kidal region beyond the city itself.

sources.name: Pravda Mali — FLA Anéfis strike, March 31, 2026

title: Russia Transfers Military Equipment to FAMa at Bamako Senou Airbase
detail: The Russian Ministry of Defence transferred a batch of military equipment to the Malian Armed Forces (FAMa) at Base 100, Senou airbase near Bamako, under the Mali-Russia strategic cooperation framework. The delivery included water purification systems, electrical generation equipment, and logistics support materiel. The transfer follows Africa Corps' full operational integration into Mali's command framework. Russia's material support for FAMa spans arms deliveries, advisers, and logistical systems — the latest in an ongoing series of transfers since the initial December 2021 deployment. The delivery underscores the deepening Russia-Mali security partnership as the AES bloc constructs its own military architecture independent of Western support.

sources.name: Pravda Mali — Russia-Mali military cooperation, March 30, 2026
ACLED — Africa Corps Mali deployment documentation
